entries.title: Old Republic Is Expansive But Not All-Powerful

content.fact: The Old Republic spans much of the galaxy but cannot instantly control distant frontier, corporate, Sith, or Mandalorian spaces.
content.injection: In Old Republic scenes, let Republic authority be real but uneven: Core bureaucracy is strong, frontier enforcement is slow, and border crises can outrun Senate process.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends Old Republic material covering Tales of the Jedi, Knights of the Old Republic, KOTOR II, SWTOR-adjacent eras, ancient Sith/Jedi state, and late Old Republic transition context before the prequel-era Republic.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon Old Republic reintroductions and from later film-era context unless stacked intentionally.

entries.title: Jedi Order Has Multiple Training Centers

content.fact: Old Republic Jedi activity can center on multiple temples, enclaves, and academy worlds depending on era.
content.injection: Do not assume all Jedi scenes must occur at the film-era Coruscant Temple; Dantooine, Tython, and other centers can be appropriate by Context.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Sith Empire Is State and Religion

content.fact: Old Republic Sith power often combines statecraft, aristocracy, military command, slavery, academy politics, and dark-side religion.
content.injection: Write Sith Empires as governments with institutions and logistics, not just scattered dark wizards. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Ancient Sith Foundations.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Sith Empire Ascendant to Eternal Empire Crisis.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Mandalorians Are Culture and War Machine

content.fact: Old Republic Mandalorians are a culture, title system, and war machine, not simply a species or generic mercenary gang.
content.injection: When Mandalorians appear, emphasize clan identity, martial prestige, armor, personal honor, and leader/title politics.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Mandalorian Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Mandalorian Wars Begin to SWTOR Class-Story Era.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

content.fact: Czerka Corporation is a useful Old Republic lever for corporate exploitation, frontier abuse, and profit-driven neutrality.
content.injection: Use Czerka when scenes need non-Sith antagonism: contracts, extraction, slavery-adjacent exploitation, and bureaucratic deniability.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Mandalorian Wars Begin to SWTOR Class-Story Era.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

content.fact: The Exchange gives KOTOR-era underworld stories organized crime infrastructure across worlds like Taris and Nar Shaddaa.
content.injection: When the Exchange is relevant, write criminal pressure through debts, bounties, informants, smugglers, and local bosses.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Civil War Begins to Malachor V Finale.

content.fact: The Rakata are an ancient precursor civilization whose surviving technology can distort KOTOR-era power balances.
content.injection: Use Rakatan ruins and artifacts as rare, dangerous legacy technology rather than normal Old Republic infrastructure.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Civil War Begins to Star Forge Destroyed.
antiLore: Do not let general model memory override the selected Legends story position.

entries.title: Sith Triumvirate Is Not Same as Sith Empire

content.fact: The Sith Triumvirate is a hidden post-KOTOR threat and should not be treated as the same political entity as SWTOR’s Sith Empire.
content.injection: Do not conflate KOTOR II’s Triumvirate with the later returned Sith Empire; they operate differently and belong to different Context windows.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Wars Begin to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Revanites can cross Republic/Sith boundaries because Revan’s legend appeals across ideological lines.
content.injection: During Revanite crisis scenes, treat Revanite loyalty as an ideological infection that can compromise both major powers.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Revanite Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Revanite Crisis to Revanite Crisis.

entries.title: Eternal Empire Has Knights, Not Sith Lords

content.fact: The Eternal Empire’s Force-users are not simply Sith Lords under another name.
content.injection: When writing Zakuul, distinguish Knights of Zakuul and Eternal Empire institutions from Sith academy hierarchy.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Eternal Empire Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Eternal Empire Crisis to Eternal Empire Crisis.

entries.title: Hutt Space Remains Old Republic Edge

content.fact: Hutt Space remains a persistent edge zone where Republic law, underworld power, and local deals collide.
content.injection: In Old Republic play, Hutt Space can plausibly host fugitives, smugglers, bounty hunters, corporate agents, and diplomats avoiding clean jurisdiction.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Jedi and Republic Are Allied, Not Identical

content.fact: The Jedi Order serves and defends the Republic but retains spiritual and institutional autonomy.
content.injection: When Jedi and Senate interests diverge, allow disagreement, delay, and moral tension rather than treating Jedi as simple Republic police.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
